response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain exposure through founding GISCollective and building web GIS applications, plus proven low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby using DLang with async I/O and fibers), which maps conceptually to the pipeline performance work this role demands. However, his professional C++ experience is dated (2014–2015 at National Instruments), he has no Rust, and his recent years have been TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than systems programming. He has no direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience, so he would be relearning the primary language and the core domain simultaneously.
strengths: Founder-level geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map) with real production users
Proven high-throughput, low-latency backend work using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Strong engineering-craft foundation: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Docker, Kubernetes
concerns: Modern C++ experience is roughly a decade old; no Rust at all
No direct routing, navigation, or map-tile data pipeline background
Recent roles centered on TypeScript/Node product work, not systems-level engineering

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ / no Rust vs. primary language requirement
Geospatial platform and GIS domain experience
